finding: nginx add_header APPENDS; /api endpoints where axum already sets Cache-Control emit a duplicate Cache-Control header, and no-cache is forced on all /api (contract change beyond the SSE goal)
Cache-Control:no-cache applied to EVERY /api response (not just SSE) forces no-cache on non-SSE GETs and duplicates the header where axum sets it — client-visible change beyond intent
Core claim TRUE (nginx consumes upstream X-Accel-Buffering and does not forward it; re-emit via add_header is correct), but add_header appends so duplicate Cache-Control results

X-Accel-Buffering:no on all /api tells the edge to stream un-buffered incl. large /api/files downloads — modest throughput cost for slow clients; not a correctness bug and arguably desirable for downloads
X-Accel-Buffering:no is applied to all /api and also leaks to the browser when no edge nginx is present; browsers ignore it, so benign

Verified NO add_header exists at http/server scope, so introducing add_header in location /api drops no inherited (HSTS/CSP/security) header today; security headers are axum-set and pass through proxy_pass untouched. Latent risk only if a parent add_header is added later

Guard is meaningful (brace-matched block; ^\s* prevents commented-out false-pass) but is documented only as a manual invocation, wired into no build/CI step, so a regression is caught only if someone remembers to run it
Regex X-Accel-Buffering\s+no\b false-passes a wrong value like `no-cache`/`no-buffering` because `-` is a word boundary — verifies presence of `no` but not that the value is exactly `no`
Brace-matching parser does not skip `#` comments or quoted strings, so a stray brace inside a comment/string within location /api would desync depth counting
readFileSync on a missing/unreadable nginx.conf throws an uncaught exception (ugly stack) instead of the friendly FAIL, though it still fails closed with a non-zero exit
Verified the guard FAILS CLOSED: null block or missing directive pushes a failure and exits 1; success path only when both directives present

worker_connections 1024 caps ~512 concurrent long-lived SSE streams per worker (client+upstream conn each) — a modest concurrent-user count could exhaust it. PRE-EXISTING (not introduced by this diff); relates to the out-of-scope SSH-lag symptom

Comment overstates default nginx buffering ('holds every frame until stream closes' — it flushes when proxy buffers fill) and the 'X-Accel-*' wildcard over-generalizes; the specific X-Accel-Buffering behavior relied on is accurate
Cache-Control line added silently, undocumented and unguarded (README/guard reference only proxy_buffering + X-Accel-Buffering), so it could regress undetected — resolved by dropping it
apiLocationBlock scans raw chars and could be fooled by a brace inside a comment/string; harmless today, latent robustness gap in an otherwise idiomatic dependency-free check
No client/server state introduced or mutated by this config/header change; angle N/A
